179	    /// Extracts the first grapheme cluster of the string.
180	    /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
181	    ///
182	    /// If `count` is specified, returns an array of the first `count`
183	    /// grapheme clusters instead.
184	    #[func]
185	    pub fn first(
186	        &self,
187	        /// Number of characters to return as an array. If omitted, returns
188	        /// a single string.
189	        #[named]
190	        count: Option<usize>,
191	    ) -> StrResult<Value> {
192	        match count {
193	            None => self
194	                .0
195	                .graphemes(true)
196	                .next()
197	                .map(|s| Str::from(s).into_value())
198	                .ok_or_else(string_is_empty),
199	            Some(count) => {
200	                let graphemes: Vec<_> = self.0.graphemes(true).take(count).collect();
201	                if graphemes.len() < count {
202	                    bail!(string_too_short());
203	                }
204	                Ok(Array::from_iter(graphemes.into_iter().map(|s| Str::from(s).into_value()))
205	                    .into_value())
206	            }
207	        }
208	    }

content.text:
Implementation complete. I added an optional `count` named parameter to `str.first()` and `str.last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`:

- No `count` → same behavior as before (returns a single `Str`, wrapped in `Value`).
- `count` given → collects that many grapheme clusters (from the front for `first`, from the back for `last`, preserving original order) into an `Array` of one-character strings, erroring if the string doesn't have enough grapheme clusters.

Coordinated with agent2 (also editing `first`/`last` in the same file for a `strip` param) via the coop messaging — shared the diff so they can layer their param on top without clobbering mine. Note: `cargo` isn't available in this environment, so I couldn't compile-check the change; I verified the code against existing patterns in the file (e.g. `bail!` interpolation, `Array::from_iter(Value)`, `IntoValue`/`Value` already imported).

Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

- No `count` → same behavior as before (returns a single `Str`, wrapped in `Value`).
- `count` given → collects that many grapheme clusters (from the front for `first`, from the back for `last`, preserving original order) into an `Array` of one-character strings, erroring if the string doesn't have enough grapheme clusters.

Coordinated with agent2 (also editing `first`/`last` in the same file for a `strip` param) via the coop messaging — shared the diff so they can layer their param on top without clobbering mine.
